reason: Perps / Trading cluster linked by [volume, solana, trading, perps, chart]

sampleTweets.text:
trashcan-sol, twice. $145 in 1 minute 8 seconds. $83 in 2 minutes 12 seconds. not my usual play but this volume was too good to ignore.

the setup: single side sol, 1 bin only. that's it.

but before anything else you need the right pool. this only works on 100/5 or 125/10 pools. anything with a 2% base fee won't give you enough to make this worthwhile.

the mechanic is simple. when your 1 bin fills and then fills back out, you're capturing roughly 2x the base fee in that single movement. on a 5% base fee pool that's around 10%, on a 10% pool you're looking at 20%+ in a single fill cycle. that's what the cards show.

volume needs to be in the 100k-200k per 5 minutes range for the bin to cycle fast enough. below that and you're just sitting there waiting.

the catch: you need to be completely locked in. eyes on the chart the whole time. when the bin fills you need to know immediately, and when it fills back out you're done, close and move on. this is not a set-and-forget play. one distraction and you miss the exit.

also prepare your mental before trying this. watching a single bin move in real time while $100+ is sitting in it is a different experience from holding a normal range position.

$228 in under 4 minutes total. gut strat for the right market conditions.

I disagree with the camp that says IBRL is the only thing that matters.

IBRL gets us very far, but consistent and verifiable ordering is table stakes for the future of perpetuals (the most valuable market in crypto).

Market makers need consistency to quote (they told me). If they don’t quote, or only quote sometimes, spreads and liquidity stay shit.

Comparing perps to spot is generally retarded. If the spreads could be the same, they would. But alas, they are not. Why? No ordering guarantees.

EvilPanda and Tiagogo explain why creating low-fee pools early destroy memecoins.

“So the coin just released, the coin just reached 200K and people start opening 0.8% pools." 

"They don't realize that if you give them such low fees, the devs has an incentive to dump the coin because it's so cheap for them.”

“If you leave them at 5%, they will dump much lower. That's what they don't understand.”

“Yeah. That’s the PVP part of degen LPing, people want to undercut and open a pool to get the fees themselves.”

“I think it happened to a few coins. I remember I was LPing right at the top when it was hitting a new ATH. Someone opened a 0.8% pool. Then in my mind, oh this is over. Then everyone went into that pool and it just rugged. No bounce at all.”

beberapa hari ini gua switch otak agent gua ke Minimax.

alasannya simpel: lebih murah dan cukup banget buat jalanin Meridian dan agent-trenching gua sehari-hari.

dengan $10 sebulan, agent gua sekarang bisa hasilin sekitar $50 per hari. 

bukan karena model-nya mahal, tapi karena agent-nya udah "belajar" dari kesalahan sebelumnya dan adapt sama kondisi market.  otak yang lebih murah bisa perform bagus kalau data dan lesson-nya bener.

sebelumnya gua pakai OpenRouter dengan pay-per-use. buat satu agent itu masih oke dan lebih fleksibel. tapi karena gua udah multi-agent dan request-nya mulai banyak, biaya pay-per-use mulai ga efisien.

gua switch ke Minimax token plan yang baru diluncurin. max 1500 request per 5 jam. dari data usage gua di OpenRouter, per 24 jam gua ngabisin sekitar 2000 request buat 2 agents.

oh dan gua juga pakai Minimax buat ngoding tipis-tipis kalau Claude lagi di peak hour buat ngirit limit.

$10 sebulan buat agent yang hasilin 50$ per hari. math-nya gut.

bikin agent yang bisa hasilin lu duit itu gampang banget. tapi cara yang paling enak buat orang non teknikal itu gini.

gua pake claude ya, kalo gaada bisa pake gemini yang cli, lagi ada diskon 3rb doang perbulan.

lu siapin flownya, datanya ambil dari mana aja.

bilang juga interview gua sampe lu ngerti 95% apa yang pengen gua buat sebenernya, jadi claude lu ga nebak nebak doang.

contoh di sini gua mau bikin listener buat agent gua, jadi dia bisa tau orang orang di dc lparmy/meteoraidn lagi pada cari pool apa.

gua suruh crosscheck dulu, kalo ada kesamaan ca di 2 server dia bakal ngirim sinyal ke agent gua buat di analyze.

ngodingnya suruh aja claude, lu tinggal approve kalo udah sesuai.

riset data presisinya dan flownya, lu bakal bisa build apa yang lu pengen.

setelah gua build beberapa agent, gua baru sadar kalo sesuatu yang paling penting adalah

dataset.

lu bisa punya LLM terbaik, agent loop yang paling clean, tooling yang paling canggih. tanpa dataset yang bener, agent lu ga bisa ngapa-ngapain yang berguna.

LLM belum bisa baca web secara native kayak manusia. tapi mereka baca markdown files dan JSON dengan sangat baik. di situlah dataset lu harus hidup. terstruktur, bersih, dan bisa diproses sama model.

kalau lu mau agent lu ngerti tentang suatu platform atau protokol, cek dulu docs-nya. terus cek apakah mereka nyediain API reference. kalau ada, itu sumber data lu. feed ke agent lu dalam format yang bisa dia proses.

makanya kenapa sekarang banyak startup yang berlomba-lomba buat "bayar" lu buat ngasih data ke mereka. 

build data pipeline-nya dulu. agent itu cuma layer di atasnya.

You don’t get to choose the use cases that force the underlying hardware to increase bandwidth or capabilities

PMF comes from catering to what people actually want, not what they say they want (revealed vs stated preference)
